content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcer- PRACTTcEs ColrrrarssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
November 28,2006
VIA U.S. MAIf, AI\ID }-ACSIMILE
The Honorable Joe l\ation
Assemblymember, S ixth Distri ct
State Capitol
Post Office Box 942849
Sacramento, CA 94249-0006
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. 4-06-209
' Govemment Code sections g1000-9i014
18i09-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulatioris
Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections
!gs
Dear Assembllnnemb er Nation :
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the use of campaign
funds under the pro,isions of the poiitical Reform Act (the ,,;;;r.l-
QUESTIONS
1' May you use campaign funds to rent or lease real property for the purpose of'
storing variQus files, documents, and other items accumulated as part of you, *ork jn the
Assembly, prior to or aft.er reaving your office on Decemb er 4,2006?
2' After leaving office, may you use campaign funds to pay for telephone service
for yourself and supporting staff while winding d;-; your campaign commrttee?
CONCLUSIONS
1' The rental of real property for the purpose of storing campaign assets ilcl,ciing
files, documents, and other items accumulatei as part of your work in the Assembly is
directly related to a political, legislatir:, ol governmentaipurpose. Accordingly, prior tcr
leaving office, you may use campaign funds to rent real property for up to one year.t a
time to store these campaign assets. upol leaving offrce, you may use surplus funds to
rent reai property for up to one year at a time to store these campaign assets as the rental
payments are for an "elected officer,s expenses.,,


File No. A-06-209
Page No. 2
2' Upon leaving office, you may use surplus funds to pay for telephone service
for yourself and supporting staff for purposes pertaining to winding down your campaign
committee as these payments are also for an "elected officeholder,f expenJes.,,
FACTS
You were first elected to the Assembly in2OO),and will be leaving office on
December 4,2006. As of December 4,2006, you will not hold any other s"tate or local
elective office. Currently, you maintain a carnpargn account from you r 2004 election to
the Assembly' You would like to use campaign t-ar from this actount to rent space fbr
the purpose of storing various files, documints, and other items accumulated as part of
your work for the Assembly and to pay communication expenses for yourself and
supporting staff as part of your work in the Assembly. In i telephorr" 
"orr,r".ration on
November 9,2006, you further explained that the communication expenses would be
incurred after leaving office. You stated that the communication expenses would be
incurred to provide telephone service for yourself and supporting staff for purposes
pertainingto winding down your campaign committee.
ANALYSIS
The use of campaign funds is governed by sections 8g5 10-g9519. Under the
general provisions governing the use of campaign funds, campaign ftnds may be used if
the expenditure bears a reasonable or direct ielaiionship to a poliiical, legislaiive, or
governmental purpose. (Section g9 512.)
Additionally, section 89517 sets out specific rules regarding the restrictions on the
use of campaigl funds for the rent or lease olreal property. Pursuint to subdivision (b)
of section 89517, campaign funds may be used to l"ur" p-p"rty for up to one year at a
time where the use of that property is directly related to pohticat, legislative, or
governmental purposes. The rental of storage space for campaign aisets is iirectly
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose . (sei witson Adviceletter,
No' 4-92-584.) Therefore, we can conclude that you .nuy use campaign funds to rent
space for the purpose of storing various files, documents, and other items accumulated as
part of your work for the Assembly, for up to one year ata time under section gg5l7.
However, campaign funds cannot be used for payment or reimbursement fbr the
rental of real property where the lessee or sublessor is a tandidate, elected offioer,
campaign treasurer, or any other individual with authority to approve the expenditure of
campaign funds, or member of his or her immediate family. (Section gosti1a1.;
Therefore, the rental agreement must be in your campaigrrcommittee,s name.
Subdivision (c) of section 89517 also provides that real property is deemed
directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose, so ltng as its use for
other purposes is only incidental to its use for political, legislative, or govlrnmental


File No. A-06-209
Page No. 3
purposes. In other words, no other use is permitted of the storage area rented by your
carrryaign committee, unless the use is incidental to the use for a political, legisiaiive, or
govemmental purpose.
Upon leaving office, the use of campaign funds is further limited as the campaign
funds become "surplus funds." (Section 895r9(a).) Under section g95i9(b), surplus
funds may only be used for the following purposes:
o The payment of outstanding campaign debts or elected officer,s expenses;
o The repayment of contributions;
o Donations allowed under section 89515 as described above;
o Contributions to a politic al party committee, provided the funds are not used to
support or oppose candidates for elective office;
o Contributions to support or oppose candidates for federal office or for elective
office outside of California; and
o Payments for professional services reasonably required to assist the committee in
the performance of its administrative functions.
In light of section 89519(b), the question becomes whether the rental of real
property for the pu{pose of storing campaign assets including files, documents, and other
items accumulated as part of your work in the Assembly is a pa5rment of an ,,elected
officer's expenses." In the wilson Advice Lelter, supra,we found, in similar
circumstances, that the use of surplus funds to "rent or lease space to store campaign
equipment" until the equipment could be sold or donated constituted the payment of an
elected officer's expenses. Consistent with the WilsonAdvice Letter, *. 
"un 
conclude
that the rental of real property for the purpose of storing campaign assets including files,
documents, and other items accumulated as part of your rork in1h" Assembly is a
payment of "elected officer's expenses" and a permissible use of surplus funds.
Notwithstanding section 89519, the use of surplus funds is still limited by section
89517. Accordingly, even after leaving office, you may only use surplus funds to rent
real property for up to one year at a time; the rental agreement must be in the name of
your campaign committee; and, the storage areamay not be used for any other purposes
unless the use is incidental to the use for a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.
While both sections 895 77 and 89519 permit the use of campaign funds and
surplus funds, respectfully, to rent storage space for campaign assets, iiis important to
note that section 84214 requires committees to be terminated within the timeiines
provided by the Commission. Unless an extension is approved, candidate controlled
committees with no "net debts outstanding" (as defined in regulations 1g531.6(d) and
18531.61(d)) must be terminated no later than nine months after the earliest of ihe date
the candidate is defeated, leaves office, or the term of office for which the committee was
formed ends. On the other hand, candidate controlled committees with ,,net debts
outstanding" must be terminated no later than twenty-four months after the earliest of the


File No. A-06-209
Page No. 4
date the candidate is defeated, leaves office, or the term of otlice for which the committee
was formed ends unless an exception is approved. (Regulation 18404.1(bX1 ) and (2).)2
Upon the termination of a carrryaign committee, the committee must "".tify that it has
ceased making expenditures and has disposed of all surplus funds. (Regulation 1g404.)
Accordingiy, once you have terminated your campaign committee you will have to
personally bear the expense of any pa5zments made for the rental of storage space.3
Turning to your second question regarding payments made for telephone service
while winding down your committee, we must again determine whether thise payments
are for an "elected officer's expenses" under section 89519. In the RubinAdvice Letter,
No. I-94-304, we concluded that a candidate for office could install a telephone line and
pay telephone bills for campaign telephone calls with campaign funds. While we did not
expressly conclude that surplus funds could be used for communication expenses, we
concluded that the candidate would have to return the phone to the commitiee or buy the
phone from the committee at fair market value, and reimburse the committee for the cost
of installing the telephone line at the time the campaign committee is closed. Clearly,
this conclusion recognized a continuing need for a candid.ateto make payments for
communication expenses while winding down his or her committee. Consistent with this
conclusion, we can determine from the facts that you have provided that using surplus
funds to pay for telephone service for yourself and supporting staff for purpoJes
pertaining to winding down your campaign committee are payments foi an ,,elected
offi ceholder's expenses."
However, we caution that section 89517(a) also prohibits the use of campaign
funds for the purchase, lease, or refurbishment of any appliance or equipment, where the
legal title resides, in whole or in part, with a candidate, Liected officer, "u-puig,
treasurer, or any other individual with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign
funds, or member of his or her immediate family. Additionally, slction g9517(c)
provides that an appliance or equipment is directly related to apolitical, legislaiive, or
goverrlmental purpose as long as its use for other purposes is iniidental to Its use for
political, legislative, or governmental purposes. Accordingly, surplus funds may be used
2 You must terminate your campaign committee within the timelines specified unless an extension
is requested and approved pursuant to regulation 1 8404. 1(0. Nothing in this letter should be construed as
supporting a request for extension.
Since you are seeking to store files and documents pertaining to your governmental work with
the Assembly, you may wish to seek the advice of the Assembly Rules bommittele regarding the possibility
of h:rning over the files and documents for retention.


File No. A-06-209
Page No. 5
to purchase equipment such as a telephone only if (1) the legal title of the equipment is in
the name of the committee, and (2) any other use is incidental to its use for polticat,
legislative, or governmental purposes.
If you have other questions on this matter,please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
. ',"'..-)
By:
Brian
Lau
Counsel, Legal Divi sion
BGLjg
